title: Elatostematoides lonchophylla
taxonomy: Elatostematoides lonchophylla belongs to the family Elatostemataceae, which is a small family of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Celastrales, a group of ancient and primitive flowering plants. The Elatostemataceae family is primarily found in tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The classification of Elatostematoides lonchophylla is also supported by its unique characteristics, such as its simple leaves and small white flowers. Overall, the classification of Elatostematoides lonchophylla reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Celastrales order.
uses: Elatostematoides lonchophylla has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. Its leaves and stems are said to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
